data.title: Apple iOS 18.1 rolls out next week, iOS 18.2 Beta now available
data.description:
Apple Intelligence (NASDAQ:AAPL) finally arrives to the iPhone as the iOS 18.1 update rolls out next week, while the iOS 18.2 Beta developer update is available now.

It remains to be seen if the arrival of Apple Intelligence features will lead to a spike in iPhone 16 demand, as several analysts have surmised.

The iOS 18.1 update will be released sometime early next week, likely on October 28. It will be compatible with all iPhone 16 models and the iPhone 15 Pro and Pro Max models. The Apple Intelligence features will initially only be available in U.S. English.

The update will include new writing tools, a new Siri interface, text and email summarizations, a "clean up" feature in Photos, improved audio transcriptions and Safari improvements.

iOS 18.2 introduces other English-speaking locations such as the U.K., Australia, Canada, New Zealand and South Africa.

The iOS 18.2 update also features artificial intelligence-powered image generation, an Image Wand that transforms rough images into detailed pictures. It also includes the anticipated Genmoji, which allows users to create their own emojis to share.

It builds on the writing tools introduced in the iOS 18.1 update, according to 9to5Mac. It also integrates OpenAI's ChatGPT into the writing tools feature and Siri.

Additional Apple Intelligence features are expected to arrive in early 2025.

Apple shares were down 2.7% by late market trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Altair Engineering jumps on report Siemens in talks to acquire
data.description:
Altair Engineering (NASDAQ:ALTR) rose 4% after a report that Siemens AG (OTCPK:SIEGY) is in talks about a possible takeover of the engineering software maker.

Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) is in working with advisers as it it discusses a potential deal for Altair (ALTR), according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Altair is exploring a sale.

The Bloomberg news comes after Reuters reported on Tuesday that the engineering software maker was exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest. Potential bidders include rival design software makers such as Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) according to the report. Reuters couldn't determine whether Cadence had approached Altair.

Earlier on Wednesday a Baird analyst wrote that a deal from Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) makes "less sense" after some recent acquisitions by Cadence.

Altair (ALTR) is scheduled to report Q3 results next Wednesday. Altair's short interest is 8.3%.

data.title: Ardmore Shipping leads tanker stocks lower as Stifel downgrades, seeing softer rates
data.description:
Ardmore Shipping (NYSE:ASC) -6.9%, International Seaways (INSW) -6%, and Scorpio Tankers (STNG) -5.2% in Wednesday's trading as Stifel downgrades the tanker stocks to Hold from Buy, anticipating softer tanker rates and asset values in 2025.

China is a major driver for future demand in nearly all commodities, and Stifel notes China's GDP growth has been slowing throughout this year, and Chinese iron ore inventories have grown 39% in the past 12 months to 135M tons and are now testing record highs; if China chooses to reverse the trend of inventory building as was the case in 2022, it likely would not bode well for Capesize rates, according to Stifel analysts led by Benjamin Nolan.

Ardmore (ASC) operates in the product and chemical tanker markets, and Nolan expects product tanker rates will be balanced in the coming year and below 2024 levels, but given its outlook for softer tanker rates and falling asset prices, shares are cut to Hold.

International Seaways (INSW) has a clean corporate structure and low leverage, Nolan says, but despite shares trading at an attractive valuation, the stock merits a Hold given its view for tanker rates and asset prices to fall below 2024 levels.

Scorpio (STNG), as one of the world's largest product tanker firms, should have better utilization and improved rates and costs spread out over a larger base relative to competitors, but despite the advantage, Nolan rates shares at Hold given its view for softer tanker rates and asset values in 2025.

In the dry bulk sector, Stifel downgrades Genco Shipping (GNK) and Star Bulk Carriers (SBLK) to Hold as the market finds its new level next year; shares trade -3.9% and -3.8%.

Given its view that dry bulk rates likely will be "softer but not bad" in 2025, Stifel expects Genco (GNK) and Star Bulk (SBLK) will generate modest cash and dividends; while shares are not expensive, the market view merits Hold ratings for both stocks until market conditions normalize.

data.title: IMF outlook: how major developed and emerging market ETFs are faring YTD
data.description:
A key gauge of stocks worldwide has gained ground during 2024, heading into what could be a modestly slower global economic-growth environment in 2025.

The MSCI All Countries World Index, covering 23 developed markets and 24 emerging markets, has risen 17% this year, compared with the 21% advance for the S&P 500 (SP500). BlackRock's (BLK) iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (NASDAQ:ACWI) has climbed nearly 18%.

The International Monetary Fund on Tuesday slightly pulled down its 2025 projection for global economic growth as it made a number of country-specific revisions. It now has a 3.2% forecast for next year, slipping from the 3.3% outlook in July. It held steady its 3.2% growth view for 2024.

Here’s how widely watched country-specific ETFs for 11 developed and emerging markets – U.S., Brazil, Canada, China, Germany, India, Japan, Mexico, South Korea, Spain, and the U.K. - were performing this year through midday Wednesday.

  • iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:IVV): +21%
  • iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (NYSEARCA:EWZ): -20%
  • iShares MSCI Canada ETF (NYSEARCA:EWC): +13%
  • iShares China Large-Cap ETF (NYSEARCA:FXI): +33%
  • iShares MSCI Germany ETF (NYSEARCA:EWG): +9.6%
  • iShares MSCI India ETF (BATS:INDA): +13%
  • iShares MSCI Japan ETF (NYSEARCA:EWJ): +4%
  • iShares MSCI Mexico ETF (NYSEARCA:EWW): -22%
  • iShares MSCI U.K. ETF (NYSEARCA:EWU): +9.7%
  • iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (NYSEARCA:EWY): - 6%
  • iShares MSCI Spain ETF (NYSEARCA:EWP): +11%

For investors who want to track the S&P 500 (SP500) via ETFs, here are a few: (VOO), (UPRO), (SPY), (IVV), (RSP), (SDS) and (SSO).

Developing story ...

data.title: Peapack-Gladstone raised to Overweight at Piper on NYC expansion; stock spikes
data.description:
Peapack-Gladstone Financial Corp. (NASDAQ:PGC) shares jumped 7.4% in Wednesday midafternoon trading after turning in better-than-expected Q3 earnings, which, in turn, drove Piper Sandler analyst Mark Fitzgibbon to upgrade the stock to Overweight from Neutral.

The New Jersey-based lender's expansion into New York City is "progressing better than we expected, helping to drive the [net interest margin] to a greater degree than we previously expected," Fitzgibbon wrote in a note.

The bank's CEO, Douglas L. Kennedy said Tuesday the expansion into the metro New York market "continues to deliver results ahead of plan," with "strong core deposit growth, continued improvement in net interest income and enhanced liquidity profile" in Q3 2024.

Elsewhere as far as tailwinds are concerned, the analyst thinks Peapack-Gladstone (PGC) "has sufficiently built reserves and proactively addressed credit challenges. And, while there is sure to be a few bumps along the way, we think the company has turned the corner on credit."

Piper's Overweight rating clashes with the SA Quant system rating of Sell and aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: Torm leads tanker stocks lower after downbeat view from Pareto
data.description:
Torm (NASDAQ:TRMD) -6.3% in Wednesday's trading following a downgrade to Hold from Buy with a ~$31 price target at Pareto Securities, which maintains its ratings across the rest of the oil tanker sector but with some reduced price targets.

"The lack of volume growth, weak refinery margins, and question marks around China have been weighing," Pareto's Eirik Haavaldsen says, lowering rate forecasts by ~30% across all tanker segments, which translates into a 10%-14% hit to FY 2024 results.

"The estimate trend turned negative this spring, and [Q4] will be another disappointment vs. original expectations," Haavaldsen writes.

Pareto continues to prefer Frontline (FRO) and Hafnia (HAFN), -5.1% and -4.1% respectively on Wednesday, which were reiterated with Buy ratings, citing "no-nonsense dividend policies and stellar track records."

Although rates have been lower than expected, Pareto says "geopolitical mayhem" could change the situation.

data.title: September videogame sales down 6% in the U.S., led by sharp drop in console spends
data.description:
U.S. videogame sales fell 6% year-over-year for the month of September, while sales figures so far this year were flat, according to a research report from Circana on Wednesday.

September sales came in at $4.37B compared with $4.68B for the same period last year, hurt by a 44% drop in hardware spending and a 3% decrease in content.

The bulk of sales as usual was in game content, which came in at $3.97B for the month. Hardware sales nearly halved to $251M, while accessory sales saw a meager 1% rise from the prior year to $217M.

So far this year, overall U.S. sales were $40.67B vs. $40.59B, helped mostly by content sales that rose 3% to $36.19B. For 2024, hardware sales saw a 30% decline to $2.55B, while accessories sales rose 7% to $1.93B.

When looking closely at hardware numbers, Circana analyst Mat Piscatella noted that the period from September 1 through October 5 saw the lowest monthly hardware spend total for a September month since 2019. He highlighted that the month saw sharp declines in Xbox Series (NASDAQ:MSFT) spending, falling 54%, while PS5 (NYSE:SONY) spending slid 45% and Nintendo Switch (OTCPK:NTDOY) was down 23%.

PlayStation 5 was the top console in unit sales and dollar terms, followed by Xbox and then Switch during the month.

Turning to game content, EA Sports FC 25 (NASDAQ:EA) was September’s best-selling video game, which reached the highest U.S. launch month dollar sales of any soccer game released to date, according to the research firm. Astro Bot was the second best-selling game of the month.

Among popular games that were released during the month were The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom, NBA 2K25, NHL 25, Marvel vs. Capcom Fighting Collection: Arcade Classics, and Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2.

Mobile game spending was $1.7B higher so far this year, while console content spending fell by $0.8 billion, as per Sensor Tower's data.

The month's top 10 mobile games by U.S. consumer spending: MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, Roblox (RBLX), Candy Crush Saga (MSFT), Last War: Survival, Whiteout Survival, Township, Brawl Stars, Coin Master, and Pokémon GO.

"The big story this month is Brawl Stars, which grew its US spend a whopping 64% month over month, moving up 4 places in this list. Brawl Stars held a groundbreaking collab with Spongebob Squarepants, which led to record-breaking US spend in a single day for the game," said Samuel Aune, the gaming insight analyst at Sensor Tower.

Related tickers: OTCPK:NTDOY, SONY, MSFT, HEAR, LOGI, CRSR, EA, TTWO, WBD, OTCPK:UBSFY, OTCPK:NCBDY, OTCPK:SQNNY, OTCPK:CCOEY, OTCPK:SGAMY, OTCPK:KONMY, OTCPK:NEXOY, SKLZ, SE, NTES, OTCPK:TCEHY, RBLX.

Retail stock: GME.

ETFs: GAMR, ESPO, NERD
